prompt: Spike this idea: a CLI tool that reads a developer's recent git history (last 7 days, current branch, all branches they've committed on) and produces a 1-paragraph status update written by Claude. Real services, not mocks — but assume no API key is available right now, so the spike code should be runnable when keys are added. Walk me through what you'd produce on day 0.

assertions.description: An assumptions list file is present in outputs.
Assumptions are categorized as must-hold / should-hold / nice-if-it-holds.
There are between 2 and 4 must-hold assumptions (or 0 if the agent justified skipping the spike).
Each must-hold assumption has a measurable, falsifiable gate.
A single-file spike exists that wires `git log` output into an Anthropic call. No package structure.
The spike imports the real Anthropic SDK and shells out to `git log` (or uses GitPython) — not mocked.
The spike file has no Pydantic models, no Click/argparse subcommand structure beyond a couple of args, no separate config files.
A `tradeoffs.md` file exists in outputs (or, if the agent skipped the spike, a `decision.md` clearly explains why).

The user-flow / information-architecture assumption is surfaced explicitly: does a 1-paragraph summary actually feel useful as a daily standup substitute? This should appear as a must-hold or should-hold assumption with a gate, or as a justification in decision.md.
An explicit decision is stated: build / pivot architecture / pivot narrative / stop.
